steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage traces a specific causal chain: water evaporates from the crust $\Rightarrow$ voids form $\Rightarrow$ oil fills those voids. We need the answer that follows directly from this mechanism when sections lose less moisture.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — will develop a thicker crust during the frying process than sections that lose more moisture.: crust thickness is never linked to moisture loss in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — will have a lower fat content after frying is completed than sections that lose more moisture.: less moisture lost $\Rightarrow$ fewer voids $\Rightarrow$ less oil absorbed $\Rightarrow$ lower fat content. This follows directly from the passage's moisture-void-oil chain. ✓
  • Choice C — will have a crust that loses its permeability less quickly during the frying process than sections that lose more moisture.: permeability rate is mentioned as a condition (the crust must remain permeable), not as a consequence of moisture loss. ✗
  • Choice D — will have a higher temperature after frying is completed than sections that lose more moisture.: temperature is not part of the moisture-void-oil pathway the passage describes. ✗
That is the correct answer.
